overall_summary: OS-level optimization for MySQL OLTP workload on Kunpeng 920 (aarch64). Applied 5 online sysctl changes and disabled irqbalance on boot. THP was already at madvise (optimal). No cpufreq governor on aarch64 (hardware-fixed). HugePages recommended but requires MySQL restart.

os_findings.note: Already set to madvise. Not always as initially documented. No change needed.
Changed from 10 to 5 (OLTP recommendation). Reduces writeback pressure on dirty page cache.
Already 0. Aligns with cpu-affinity manual IRQ binding.
Default (no zone reclaim). Suitable for NUMA with manual binding.
Changed from 1024 to 65535. Supports higher connection burst for 10K+ TPS workload.
Changed from 1000 to 10000. Increases NIC ingress queue depth.
Changed from 256 to 4096. Accommodates higher SYN backlog under load.
Changed from 2 to 1. Enables TIME_WAIT socket reuse for client connections.
Service already inactive (stopped by cpu-affinity). Disabled on boot to prevent re-enable after reboot overriding manual IRQ affinity.
Kunpeng 920 (aarch64) does not expose cpufreq subsystem. Frequency is hardware-managed at maximum. No action needed.
NVMe device (nvme0n1, MySQL data) uses none. SAS HDDs use mq-deadline. Both correct.
102400 exceeds the 65535 threshold. No change needed.
MySQL buffer pool = 24G > 16GB threshold. Explicit HugePages could reduce TLB misses. Requires MySQL restart and host hugepage allocation. See restart_required_actions.
Default 2MB pages are adequate for this workload. 1G pages not needed.

online_change_actions.rationale: Reduce dirty page threshold from 10% to 5%. For OLTP with O_DIRECT, fewer dirty pages means less writeback pressure, improving read latency under mixed workloads.
Increase listen backlog from 1024 to 65535. At 10K+ TPS with 64 threads, default backlog can cause connection drops under burst.
Increase NIC ingress packet queue from 1000 to 10000. Prevents packet drops under high network throughput.
Increase SYN backlog from 256 to 4096. Supports higher connection establishment rate.
Enable TIME_WAIT socket reuse. Reduces port exhaustion under high connection rate from client.
IRQ affinity already manually configured by cpu-affinity-optimization. irqbalance would override manual bindings if restarted. Service is already stopped.

restart_required_actions.rationale: MySQL buffer pool is 24G (>16GB threshold). Explicit HugePages reduce TLB pressure and improve memory access efficiency. THP is already at madvise; explicit HugePages provide deterministic page size.
steps: Restart MySQL service to use hugepages via MADV_HUGEPAGE
restart_required_actions.deferred_reason: MySQL restart required; not confirmed as allowed in current cycle

rollback.config_file_rollback: Remove the '# ===== OS Optimization for MySQL OLTP =====' section from /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf and run sysctl -p

validation_plan.command: sysctl vm.dirty_ratio net.core.somaxconn net.core.netdev_max_backlog net.ipv4.tcp_max_syn_backlog net.ipv4.tcp_tw_reuse
validation_plan.expected: All values match the new settings

validation_plan.check: Verify irqbalance stays off after reboot
Benchmark TPS impact (comparison with pre-OS-opt baseline)
validation_plan.method: Run same sysbench workload as app-config optimization (64 threads) and compare TPS
validation_plan.expected: No regression; minor improvement from reduced dirty_ratio and network backlog tuning

container_boundary_notes: MySQL is running on host (PID 449168), not inside Docker. Docker containers present on host are unrelated shells. All OS-level changes apply directly to MySQL environment. No container boundary restrictions.
